title: by-value capture + acyclic-data enforcement
manager: When an expression or lambda captures data, it captures by value, and the language enforces that captured data is acyclic — no shared mutable references, no reference cycles that make equality and serialization undecidable. This is what keeps FSL values comparable, hashable, and replayable: a captured value is a snapshot, not a live pointer into somebody else's state that changes under you between two reads.
cs: by-value capture; acyclic-data enforcement (compile/runtime guard); underpins intensional equality (W6.28), serialization, and the defunctionalization hash (W6.27).
